title: Evangelions Length
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion's Length             Quote Post by Dave » Fri Oct 15, 2004 8:57 pm Exactly how many days is it from the moment Shinji arrives in Tokyo-3 until Asuka's famous "How disgusting..." line? I would also like to find out specific dates if at all possible (of course, that is assuming that Anno even had dates worked out).

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Fri Oct 15, 2004 9:11 pm i don't even think it mentions the dates in it anywhere.

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Fri Oct 15, 2004 10:14 pm Oh gawd...i could only guess. The most resonable one seems like it happened within half a year....even less.

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Sat Oct 16, 2004 12:39 am Yea I would guesstimate about 6 months or so.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Sat Oct 16, 2004 1:06 am A few months ago, I tried to work out which month-range the episodes could have taken place during by looking at birthdays and all. I've figured it out. Shinji is the latest person, on June 6th, born in a year with a confirmed birth year. That way, we know that the time-frame of Evangelion must be after June 6th, 2015. Japanese school holidays happen on March 7th - April 1st, July 15th - September 1st and December 22nd - January 9th. We never see a school holiday during the episodes, and the time frame must be longer than a month (especially considering Shinji's time as LCL in Unit-01), so it couldn't be in the June 6th - July 15th period. I deduct, then, that the episodes happened some time between September 1st to December 22 of 2015. Please, dear God, let me be right.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sat Oct 16, 2004 3:43 am MAGI wrote: A few months ago, I tried to work out which month-range the episodes could have taken place during by looking at birthdays and all. I've figured it out. Shinji is the latest person, on June 6th, born in a year with a confirmed birth year. That way, we know that the time-frame of Evangelion must be after June 6th, 2015. Japanese school holidays happen on March 7th - April 1st, July 15th - September 1st and December 22nd - January 9th. We never see a school holiday during the episodes, and the time frame must be longer than a month (especially considering Shinji's time as LCL in Unit-01), so it couldn't be in the June 6th - July 15th period. I deduct, then, that the episodes happened some time between September 1st to December 22 of 2015. Please, dear God, let me be right.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Oct 16, 2004 4:46 am Shinji is summoned to Tokyo 3 sometime in the summer of 2015 and #26 says Third Impact has occured in 2016; so the story seems to take place over the last six months or so of 2015. As for when Shinji comes back to the real world, thats hard to say; because time has no meaning in Instrumentality, it could have taken 5 minutes or 500 years, for all we know, for Shinji to make his choice to reject Complementation and come back. Also, regardless of that, there are hints that Shinji was alone on that beach for some substantial, but indefinite, length of time before Asuka turns up, so it is impossible to nail down exactly when 'I need you' takes place, relative to the rest of the story.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Sat Oct 16, 2004 3:44 pm well...i guess we can all agree that the episodes span approximately 6 months...

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Sat Oct 16, 2004 4:21 pm i thought it was during a year, oh well, i sit corrected

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Oct 16, 2004 4:37 pm I tried to figure this out once while watching Eva, but only got a headache. I think the time line was kept vague on prupose, but from what I gathered, I thought somewhere between six and eight months.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 16, 2004 5:47 pm Also, regardless of that, there are hints that Shinji was alone on that beach for some substantial, but indefinite, length of time before Asuka turns up, so it is impossible to nail down exactly when 'I need you' takes place, relative to the rest of the story.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sat Oct 16, 2004 6:10 pm Misato's cross is nailed to a tree, so I guess Shinji was there for at least a little while...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 16, 2004 7:12 pm I don't know about "hints" (plural), but one hint is that the cross has rust on it when we see it in "I Need You".

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Sat Oct 16, 2004 7:21 pm Though I don't know why it was cut, the original ending showed Shinji making graves for each of the characters, before finding Asuka lying on the beach, the shot of Misato's cross was one of them. In yet another alternative ending, Shinji was meant to be shown holding a hand, lying on the beach. As the camera pans out, it would be discovered that the hand wasn't attached to anything, just a hand and an arm. Perhaps it was meant to show that Asuka had imagined her return, but rather than bandages, she was just an arm? :shock: Thankfully, they didn't use that ending, would've scarred me for life.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 16, 2004 7:43 pm There is more about the alternate endings here .

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Oct 16, 2004 9:21 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: Also, regardless of that, there are hints that Shinji was alone on that beach for some substantial, but indefinite, length of time before Asuka turns up, so it is impossible to nail down exactly when 'I need you' takes place, relative to the rest of the story.

[]:             Quote Post by Esion » Sat Oct 16, 2004 9:29 pm MAGI wrote: A few months ago, I tried to work out which month-range the episodes could have taken place during by looking at birthdays and all. I've figured it out. Shinji is the latest person, on June 6th, born in a year with a confirmed birth year. That way, we know that the time-frame of Evangelion must be after June 6th, 2015. Japanese school holidays happen on March 7th - April 1st, July 15th - September 1st and December 22nd - January 9th. We never see a school holiday during the episodes, and the time frame must be longer than a month (especially considering Shinji's time as LCL in Unit-01), so it couldn't be in the June 6th - July 15th period. I deduct, then, that the episodes happened some time between September 1st to December 22 of 2015. Please, dear God, let me be right.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sat Oct 16, 2004 11:38 pm My god, I could have gone the rest of my life without even wondering what those posts at the End of Eva were. And this is after what, 50 veiwings? I love this show. ^^

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Sun Oct 17, 2004 12:10 am Esion wrote: Isn't there some sort of Mayan belief that the world will end (or enter some kind of transition) in december of the year 2015?

[]:             Quote Post by Esion » Sun Oct 17, 2004 12:32 am Well nevermind then.
